enrichment.short_description: Automad is a free, open-source, flat-file CMS built in PHP that requires no database, using simple text files for content and its own templating toolkit.
enrichment.seo_title: Automad: Free Flat-File CMS Without a Database
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Automad is a free, open-source, database-free flat-file CMS built in PHP, with a page-tree structure and a lightweight in-browser editor.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Automad is a free, open-source flat-file content management system built in PHP, developed by Marc Anton Dahmen and maintained as a community open-source project on GitHub. Unlike traditional CMS platforms such as WordPress, Automad stores all content in plain text files and a simple folder structure rather than a database, so a site's content can be edited, version-controlled, and moved without needing MySQL or similar database software. It uses its own lightweight templating toolkit for building themes, along with a lightweight in-browser editing interface for non-technical content editors. Automad organizes content in a page-tree structure that mirrors a site's navigation, and supports custom fields, multilingual sites, and a searchable file/asset system. Because it has no database dependency, Automad sites are simple to back up, deploy, and host on standard PHP web hosting without special server configuration. The software is distributed under the MIT license with no cost or licensing fees. Automad is best suited to developers and small businesses building brochure sites, blogs, or portfolios who want a lightweight, git-friendly, dependency-light CMS alternative to database-driven systems, particularly in cases where hosting simplicity and long-term content portability matter more than the extensive plugin ecosystems of larger CMS platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Automad is a free, open-source, flat-file content management system built in PHP by developer Marc Anton Dahmen. Instead of relying on a database like MySQL, Automad stores all page content as plain text files organized in a simple folder structure.
This flat-file architecture makes Automad sites easy to back up, version-control with Git, and move between hosting environments, since there is no database to migrate or configure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Automad organizes content in a page-tree structure that mirrors site navigation, and includes a lightweight in-browser editing interface so non-technical users can update content without touching the file system directly.
The platform uses its own templating toolkit for building custom themes, and supports custom fields, multilingual content, and a built-in file/asset management system.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Automad is free and open source under the MIT license, with no licensing fees or paid tiers. Users only pay for their own PHP-capable web hosting.

features.title: Flat-file storage
features.description: Content is stored as plain text files instead of a database, simplifying backup and portability.

features.title: No database required
features.description: Runs on standard PHP hosting without MySQL or similar database setup.

features.title: In-browser editor
features.description: Lightweight visual editor for content editors to update pages without touching files directly.

features.title: Page-tree structure
features.description: Content is organized in a tree that mirrors the site's navigation hierarchy.

features.title: Custom templating toolkit
features.description: Build custom themes using Automad's own lightweight templating system.

features.title: Multilingual support
features.description: Build and manage content in multiple languages within one Automad site.

pros: Completely free and open source under the MIT license
No database dependency simplifies hosting, backup, and version control
Lightweight and fast, well-suited to small-to-medium brochure sites
Git-friendly since content lives in plain text files
cons: Smaller plugin/theme ecosystem than database-driven CMS platforms like WordPress
Flat-file architecture can be less efficient at very large scale with thousands of pages
Requires PHP hosting knowledge to install and maintain

faqs.question: Is Automad free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Automad is free and open source under the MIT license with no licensing fees.

faqs.question: Does Automad require a database?
faqs.answer: No, Automad is a flat-file CMS that stores content in plain text files instead of a database.

faqs.question: Can non-technical users edit content in Automad?
faqs.answer: Yes, Automad includes a lightweight in-browser editing interface for updating content without editing files directly.

faqs.question: Is Automad good for large websites?
faqs.answer: Automad is best suited to small-to-medium sites; very large sites with thousands of pages may be better served by a database-driven CMS.

use_cases.title: Brochure and portfolio websites
use_cases.description: Building simple, fast-loading marketing or portfolio sites without database overhead.

use_cases.title: Developer-friendly content sites
use_cases.description: Managing content as plain text files for easy Git version control and deployment.

enrichment.short_description: Automatisch is an open-source workflow automation platform, self-hostable as an alternative to Zapier or Make, connecting apps without writing code.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (self-hosted); Cloud plans from paid tiers
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Automatisch is an open-source, self-hostable workflow automation tool connecting apps with no-code triggers and actions, an alternative to Zapier.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Automatisch is an open-source business automation platform positioned as a self-hostable alternative to proprietary tools like Zapier and Make. It lets users connect different apps and automate repetitive workflows using trigger-and-action flows without writing code, and is distributed under an open-core model: the core platform is free and open source (licensed under a fair-source-style license), with an official managed Cloud version and a paid Enterprise self-hosted edition available for teams that want additional support, integrations, or hosting convenience. Because the core software can be self-hosted on a company's own infrastructure, Automatisch appeals to organizations with data privacy or compliance requirements that make sending data through third-party SaaS automation platforms undesirable. It supports connecting popular business apps and services via pre-built integrations, with new connectors added by an active open-source contributor community. Automatisch is aimed at developers, IT teams, and privacy-conscious businesses that want Zapier-style automation capabilities while retaining full control over where their workflow data is processed and stored, as well as teams looking to avoid per-task or per-zap pricing common among proprietary automation SaaS platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Automatisch is an open-source workflow automation platform built as a self-hostable alternative to tools like Zapier and Make. It lets users build no-code automations that connect different apps through trigger-and-action flows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform supports connecting business apps via pre-built integrations maintained by an active open-source community, with a no-code flow builder for defining triggers and actions.

Because the core is open source and self-hostable, teams can run Automatisch entirely on their own infrastructure, keeping workflow data under their own control rather than routing it through a third-party SaaS platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Automatisch's core platform is free to self-host. The company also offers an official managed Cloud version and a paid Enterprise self-hosted edition with additional support and features for teams that want a more turnkey experience.

features.title: No-code automation builder
features.description: Build trigger-and-action workflows connecting apps without writing code.

features.title: Self-hostable core
features.description: Run the open-source platform on your own infrastructure for full data control.

features.title: App integrations
features.description: Pre-built connectors for popular business apps, maintained by the open-source community.

features.title: Managed Cloud option
features.description: Official hosted Cloud version for teams that don't want to self-host.

features.title: Enterprise edition
features.description: Paid self-hosted tier with added support and features for larger organizations.

pros: Free, open-source core that can be self-hosted for full data control
Avoids per-task/per-zap pricing common with proprietary automation SaaS tools
Managed Cloud option available for teams that don't want to self-host
Actively developed with community-contributed integrations
cons: Integration library is smaller than established platforms like Zapier or Make
Self-hosting requires infrastructure setup and maintenance
Enterprise features and support require a paid tier

faqs.question: Is Automatisch free?
faqs.answer: The core platform is free and open source to self-host; a managed Cloud version and paid Enterprise edition are also available.

faqs.question: Can Automatisch be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Automatisch is designed to be self-hostable on your own infrastructure.

faqs.question: What is Automatisch an alternative to?
faqs.answer: Automatisch is positioned as an open-source alternative to workflow automation tools like Zapier and Make.

faqs.question: Does Automatisch require coding?
faqs.answer: No, it uses a no-code flow builder for defining triggers and actions between apps.

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious workflow automation
use_cases.description: Organizations with data privacy requirements that need to self-host their automation workflows.

use_cases.title: Cost-effective app automation
use_cases.description: Teams wanting to avoid per-task pricing common in proprietary automation SaaS tools.

enrichment.short_description: Autumn is a billing infrastructure layer built on top of Stripe that lets SaaS companies manage pricing plans, feature entitlements, and usage-based billing via API.
enrichment.seo_title: Autumn: Pricing & Billing Infrastructure on Stripe
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Autumn is a billing layer on top of Stripe for managing SaaS pricing plans, feature entitlements, and usage-based billing without custom billing code.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Autumn (useautumn.com) is a billing infrastructure platform built on top of Stripe, designed to let SaaS companies manage pricing plans, feature entitlements, and usage-based billing logic without building custom billing code in-house. Rather than replacing Stripe, Autumn sits as a layer above it, letting engineering teams define pricing plans and feature access rules through a dashboard and SDK, then handles plan changes, upgrades/downgrades, proration, usage tracking, and entitlement checks (e.g., 'has this customer hit their usage limit?') via API calls, reducing the amount of custom billing logic teams need to write and maintain. It targets developers and founders at early-stage and growth-stage SaaS companies who want to iterate quickly on pricing models — including seat-based, usage-based, and tiered/credit-based pricing — without repeatedly rewriting billing infrastructure each time pricing changes. Autumn offers a free tier for getting started, with paid plans scaling based on usage/revenue processed through the platform for companies that need higher limits, priority support, or advanced features. Because it still uses Stripe as the underlying payment processor, customers retain compliance and payment-processing guarantees from Stripe while gaining a simpler, more flexible way to model and change pricing over time.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Autumn is a billing infrastructure platform built on top of Stripe, founded in 2024 and headquartered in San Francisco, California. It lets SaaS companies manage pricing plans, feature entitlements, and usage-based billing without building custom billing logic from scratch.

Rather than replacing Stripe, Autumn acts as a layer above it — Stripe still processes payments, while Autumn manages the pricing plan configuration, entitlement checks, and billing logic that would otherwise require custom engineering work.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Autumn lets teams define pricing plans (seat-based, usage-based, tiered, or credit-based) through a dashboard and SDK, and handles plan upgrades/downgrades, proration, and usage tracking automatically via API.
Feature entitlement checks let applications query whether a customer has access to a feature or has hit a usage limit, without teams writing and maintaining that billing logic themselves.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Autumn offers a free tier for getting started, with paid plans scaling based on usage or revenue processed through the platform for companies needing higher limits, priority support, or advanced features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Autumn is aimed at developers and founders at early-stage and growth-stage SaaS companies who want to iterate quickly on pricing models without repeatedly rebuilding billing infrastructure.

features.title: Pricing plan management
features.description: Define seat-based, usage-based, tiered, or credit-based pricing plans through a dashboard and SDK.

features.title: Feature entitlements
features.description: Check whether a customer has access to a feature or has hit a usage limit via API.

features.title: Automated plan changes
features.description: Handles upgrades, downgrades, and proration logic automatically.

features.title: Usage tracking
features.description: Tracks metered usage for usage-based billing plans.

features.title: Built on Stripe
features.description: Uses Stripe as the underlying payment processor for compliance and reliability.

pros: Reduces custom billing engineering work for SaaS teams
Free tier available to get started
Built on Stripe, retaining Stripe's payment compliance and reliability
Supports multiple pricing models (seat, usage, tiered, credit-based)
cons: As a newer platform (founded 2024), long-term ecosystem maturity is still developing
Requires a Stripe account since Autumn is a layer on top of Stripe rather than a standalone processor
Paid tier pricing details require checking current plans as the product evolves

faqs.question: Does Autumn replace Stripe?
faqs.answer: No, Autumn is a billing layer built on top of Stripe; Stripe still processes payments while Autumn manages pricing plans and entitlements.

faqs.question: Is Autumn free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Autumn offers a free tier to get started, with paid plans scaling based on usage or revenue processed.

faqs.question: What pricing models does Autumn support?
faqs.answer: Autumn supports seat-based, usage-based, tiered, and credit-based pricing models.

faqs.question: Who is Autumn for?
faqs.answer: Autumn is aimed at developers and founders at SaaS companies who want to manage and iterate on pricing without custom billing code.

use_cases.title: SaaS pricing plan management
use_cases.description: Defining and updating pricing plans without rewriting billing code each time.

use_cases.title: Usage-based billing
use_cases.description: Tracking metered usage and enforcing feature limits via API.

enrichment.short_description: Avalara is a cloud-based tax compliance platform that automates sales tax, VAT, and cross-border tax calculation, filing, and remittance for businesses.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Avalara automates sales tax, VAT, and cross-border tax calculation, filing, and remittance for businesses of all sizes, integrating with 1,200+ systems.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Avalara is a cloud-based tax compliance platform founded in 2004 by Rory Rawlings and Jared Vogt, headquartered in Seattle, Washington, with more than 5,000 employees. It automates tax determination, calculation, filing, and remittance across sales tax, VAT/GST, and cross-border trade compliance for businesses selling in the US and internationally. Avalara's core products include AvaTax for real-time sales tax calculation, Returns for automated tax return filing and remittance, and cross-border tools that calculate duties and tariffs for international shipments. The platform integrates with more than 1,200 accounting, ERP, and e-commerce systems, including Shopify, NetSuite, Salesforce, QuickBooks, and Microsoft Dynamics, so tax logic can be embedded directly into existing checkout and billing workflows rather than calculated manually. Avalara went public on the NYSE in 2018 and was later taken private in 2022 by Vista Equity Partners in a roughly $8.4 billion acquisition. Pricing is not published on Avalara's website and is customized based on transaction volume, jurisdictions, and which products a business needs; prospective customers must request a quote. Avalara serves businesses of all sizes, from small e-commerce sellers to large multinational enterprises, that need to stay compliant with constantly changing tax rates and rules across thousands of US and international jurisdictions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Avalara is a cloud-based tax compliance platform founded in 2004 and headquartered in Seattle, Washington, with more than 5,000 employees. It automates sales tax, VAT/GST, and cross-border trade compliance for businesses operating in the US and internationally.
Avalara went public on the NYSE in 2018 and was taken private in 2022 by Vista Equity Partners in an acquisition valued at roughly $8.4 billion.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: AvaTax provides real-time sales tax calculation at checkout, while Avalara Returns automates tax return filing and remittance across jurisdictions. Cross-border tools calculate duties and tariffs for international shipments.

Avalara integrates with more than 1,200 accounting, ERP, and e-commerce platforms, including Shopify, NetSuite, Salesforce, QuickBooks, and Microsoft Dynamics, embedding tax calculation directly into existing billing and checkout workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Avalara does not publish pricing on its website. Costs are customized based on transaction volume, the number of tax jurisdictions covered, and which Avalara products a business needs; prospective customers must request a quote.

features.title: AvaTax Real-Time Calculation
features.description: Calculates accurate sales tax rates at checkout across US and international jurisdictions.

features.title: Automated Tax Filing (Returns)
features.description: Automates tax return preparation, filing, and remittance to tax authorities.

features.title: Cross-Border Compliance
features.description: Calculates duties, tariffs, and import taxes for international shipments.

features.title: 1,200+ Integrations
features.description: Connects with major accounting, ERP, and e-commerce platforms including Shopify, NetSuite, and QuickBooks.

features.title: Exemption Certificate Management

pros: Extensive integration library covering major accounting, ERP, and e-commerce platforms
Automates complex, constantly-changing tax rules across thousands of jurisdictions
Backed by two decades of operation and a large enterprise customer base
Covers both domestic sales tax and international cross-border compliance
cons: Pricing is not published and requires a custom sales quote
Implementation and integration setup can require significant configuration for complex businesses
Cost may be high for very small businesses with simple, single-jurisdiction tax needs

faqs.question: What does Avalara do?
faqs.answer: Avalara automates sales tax, VAT, and cross-border tax calculation, filing, and remittance for businesses selling domestically and internationally.

faqs.question: How much does Avalara cost?
faqs.answer: Avalara does not publish pricing; costs are customized based on transaction volume, jurisdictions, and products needed, requiring a quote from sales.

faqs.question: Does Avalara integrate with e-commerce platforms?
faqs.answer: Yes, Avalara integrates with more than 1,200 systems including Shopify, NetSuite, Salesforce, QuickBooks, and Microsoft Dynamics.

faqs.question: Is Avalara publicly traded?
faqs.answer: Avalara was publicly traded on the NYSE starting in 2018 but was taken private in 2022 by Vista Equity Partners.

use_cases.description: Automatically calculating and applying correct sales tax at checkout across jurisdictions.

use_cases.title: Multinational tax compliance
use_cases.description: Managing VAT, GST, and cross-border duties for international sales and shipping.

enrichment.short_description: Avast is a cybersecurity company offering antivirus, VPN, and online privacy software for consumers and businesses, now part of Gen Digital.
enrichment.seo_title: Avast: Antivirus, VPN & Online Security Software
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Avast offers free and paid antivirus, VPN, and privacy protection software for Windows, Mac, Android, and iOS, now part of Gen Digital.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Avast is a cybersecurity software company founded in 1988 by Eduard Kučera and Pavel Baudiš in Prague, Czechoslovakia (now the Czech Republic), where it remains headquartered. Avast is best known for Avast Free Antivirus, one of the most widely used free antivirus products globally, alongside paid products covering premium antivirus/internet security, a VPN (Avast SecureLine VPN), a password manager, and performance optimization tools (Avast Cleanup). The company also offers Avast One, a consolidated all-in-one subscription bundling antivirus, VPN, and privacy/identity protection features. Avast software runs on Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS. The company went public on the London Stock Exchange in 2018 and merged with NortonLifeLock in 2022 to form Gen Digital Inc., a publicly traded cybersecurity conglomerate that also owns Norton, LifeLock, and CCleaner; Avast continues to operate as one of Gen Digital's core consumer security brands. Avast has faced past scrutiny over data practices at its former Jumpshot analytics subsidiary (shut down in 2020) and paid an FTC settlement in 2024 related to historical data-sharing practices, which the company addressed through subsequent policy and product changes. Avast serves individual consumers seeking free or affordable malware protection, as well as small businesses through Avast Business product lines.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Avast is a cybersecurity software company founded in 1988 by Eduard Kučera and Pavel Baudiš in Prague, where it remains headquartered. It is best known for Avast Free Antivirus, one of the most widely used free antivirus products worldwide.

In 2022, Avast merged with NortonLifeLock to form Gen Digital Inc., a publicly traded cybersecurity conglomerate that also owns Norton, LifeLock, and CCleaner, with Avast continuing to operate as one of its core consumer security brands.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Avast's product line spans free and premium antivirus/internet security, Avast SecureLine VPN, a password manager, and Avast Cleanup performance optimization tools, alongside Avast One, an all-in-one subscription bundling antivirus, VPN, and identity protection.
Avast software is available across Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS, and the company also offers Avast Business product lines for small and medium businesses.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Avast offers a free antivirus tier for individual consumers, with paid subscriptions for premium security suites, VPN, and Avast One bundles. Pricing varies by product and region, with annual subscription plans being the most common billing structure.

features.description: Available across Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS.

Broad product ecosystem covering antivirus, VPN, and privacy tools
Available across all major desktop and mobile platforms
Backed by Gen Digital, a large publicly traded cybersecurity company
cons: Past controversy over data-sharing practices via its former Jumpshot subsidiary led to an FTC settlement in 2024
Free tier includes upsell prompts for paid products
Premium features require separate or bundled paid subscriptions

faqs.question: Is Avast free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Avast offers a free antivirus tier, with paid subscriptions available for premium security, VPN, and identity protection features.

faqs.question: Who owns Avast?
faqs.answer: Avast is part of Gen Digital Inc., formed by Avast's 2022 merger with NortonLifeLock, which also owns Norton, LifeLock, and CCleaner.

faqs.question: What platforms does Avast support?
faqs.answer: Avast software is available for Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS.

faqs.question: Did Avast have data privacy issues?
faqs.answer: Yes, Avast's former Jumpshot analytics subsidiary (shut down in 2020) faced scrutiny over data practices, and Avast reached an FTC settlement in 2024 related to historical data-sharing.

enrichment.short_description: AvidXchange is an accounts payable and payment automation platform that helps mid-market businesses digitize invoice processing and B2B payments.
enrichment.seo_title: AvidXchange: AP Automation & B2B Payments Platform
enrichment.seo_meta_description: AvidXchange automates accounts payable and B2B payments for mid-market businesses, integrating with 220+ accounting and ERP systems.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: AvidXchange is a publicly traded (NASDAQ: AVDX) accounts payable and B2B payment automation platform founded in 2000 by Michael Praeger, Walter Hoff, and William Fung, headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, with more than 1,500 employees. The company digitizes and automates the invoice-to-pay process for mid-market businesses, including invoice capture and data extraction, approval workflow routing, purchase order matching, and electronic payment processing to suppliers. AvidXchange integrates with more than 220 accounting and ERP systems, and offers industry-specific solutions tailored to sectors such as real estate, construction, financial services, media, homeowners associations (HOAs), and healthcare. The platform processes payments through multiple methods, including virtual cards, ACH, and checks, and provides suppliers with a portal to track payment status. AvidXchange went public via IPO on NASDAQ in 2021. Pricing is not publicly listed and is provided through a custom quote based on transaction volume, integrations needed, and company size. It is best suited to mid-market companies processing a high volume of vendor invoices and payments that want to reduce manual AP processing time, improve visibility into cash flow, and eliminate paper-based invoice workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: AvidXchange is a publicly traded accounts payable and B2B payment automation platform (NASDAQ: AVDX), founded in 2000 and headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, with more than 1,500 employees. It digitizes the invoice-to-pay process for mid-market businesses.
AvidXchange went public via IPO on NASDAQ in 2021 and has grown into one of the larger dedicated AP automation providers in the US mid-market segment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform automates invoice capture and data extraction, approval workflow routing, and purchase order matching, alongside electronic payment processing via virtual cards, ACH, and checks. Suppliers get access to a portal for tracking payment status.
AvidXchange integrates with more than 220 accounting and ERP systems and offers industry-specific solutions for real estate, construction, financial services, media, HOAs, and healthcare.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: AvidXchange does not publish pricing on its website. Costs are determined through a custom quote based on transaction volume, required integrations, and company size.

faqs.answer: AvidXchange automates accounts payable and B2B payment processes, including invoice capture, approval routing, and electronic payments to suppliers.

faqs.question: How much does AvidXchange cost?
faqs.answer: AvidXchange does not publish pricing; costs are determined through a custom quote based on transaction volume and integrations needed.

faqs.question: What industries does AvidXchange serve?
faqs.answer: AvidXchange offers industry-specific solutions for real estate, construction, financial services, media, homeowners associations, and healthcare.

faqs.question: Is AvidXchange publicly traded?
faqs.answer: Yes, AvidXchange trades on NASDAQ under the ticker AVDX after its 2021 IPO.

enrichment.short_description: Aviso is an AI-powered revenue intelligence and forecasting platform that helps sales teams predict pipeline outcomes and optimize deal execution.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Aviso is an AI-powered revenue intelligence platform for sales forecasting, pipeline management, and deal execution, used by enterprise sales teams.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Aviso is an AI-powered revenue intelligence and sales forecasting platform founded in 2012 by Andrew Abrahams, Nagaraj Kuppuswamy, and K.V. Rao, headquartered in Redwood City, California. It uses AI and machine learning to analyze CRM data, sales activity, and rep behavior to generate sales forecasts, flag at-risk deals, and surface pipeline insights intended to be more accurate than manually assembled spreadsheet forecasts. Core capabilities include AI-driven revenue forecasting, deal inspection and risk scoring, conversation intelligence for analyzing sales calls, pipeline management dashboards, and guided selling recommendations that suggest next-best actions for reps. Aviso integrates with major CRM platforms such as Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamics, along with email, calendar, and conferencing tools used in the sales process. The company reports serving large enterprise sales organizations across industries including technology, financial services, and manufacturing, with customers such as Dell Technologies, Splunk, and Genesys cited in various case studies. Pricing is not publicly disclosed and is provided through a custom sales quote based on team size and modules needed. Aviso is best suited to enterprise and mid-market sales operations and revenue leadership teams that manage complex, multi-stage B2B sales pipelines and want AI-assisted forecasting accuracy and deal risk visibility beyond what native CRM reporting provides.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Aviso is an AI-powered revenue intelligence and forecasting platform founded in 2012 and headquartered in Redwood City, California. It analyzes CRM data, sales activity, and rep behavior to generate sales forecasts and surface pipeline risk beyond what manual spreadsheet forecasting typically catches.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Aviso's core capabilities include AI-driven revenue forecasting, deal inspection and risk scoring, conversation intelligence for analyzing sales calls, and pipeline management dashboards.

Guided selling recommendations suggest next-best actions for reps, and the platform integrates with major CRM systems like Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamics, plus email, calendar, and conferencing tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Aviso is aimed at enterprise and mid-market sales operations and revenue leadership teams managing complex, multi-stage B2B sales pipelines who need AI-assisted forecasting accuracy and deal risk visibility.

features.description: Generates sales forecasts using AI analysis of CRM and activity data.

features.title: Deal Risk Scoring
features.description: Flags at-risk deals in the pipeline based on engagement and activity signals.

features.title: Conversation Intelligence
features.description: Analyzes sales calls for insights into deal health and rep performance.

features.title: Pipeline Management Dashboards
features.description: Visual dashboards for tracking pipeline health and progress.

features.title: Guided Selling
features.description: AI-suggested next-best actions for sales reps on active deals.

features.title: CRM Integrations
features.description: Connects with Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics, and other sales tools.

pros: AI-driven forecasting can improve accuracy over manual spreadsheet forecasting
Deep integration with major CRM platforms
Used by large enterprise sales organizations across multiple industries
Combines forecasting, deal risk, and conversation intelligence in one platform
cons: Pricing is not publicly available and requires a custom sales quote
Primarily built for enterprise/mid-market complexity, which may be more than small sales teams need
Effectiveness depends on data quality and consistent CRM/activity logging by reps

faqs.question: What does Aviso do?
faqs.answer: Aviso is an AI-powered revenue intelligence platform that generates sales forecasts, flags at-risk deals, and provides pipeline visibility for sales teams.

faqs.question: How much does Aviso cost?
faqs.answer: Aviso does not publish pricing; costs are provided through a custom sales quote based on team size and modules needed.

faqs.question: Does Aviso integrate with Salesforce?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aviso integrates with major CRM platforms including Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamics.

faqs.question: Who uses Aviso?
faqs.answer: Aviso is used by enterprise and mid-market sales operations and revenue leadership teams managing complex B2B sales pipelines.

enrichment.short_description: Avoma is an AI meeting assistant and revenue intelligence platform that records, transcribes, and analyzes sales and customer-facing calls.
enrichment.seo_title: Avoma: AI Meeting Assistant & Revenue Intelligence
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Avoma records, transcribes, and analyzes sales and customer meetings with AI, offering conversation intelligence, coaching, and CRM integrations.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Avoma is an AI-powered meeting assistant and revenue intelligence platform founded in 2017 by Rammohan Reddy Gaddam and Vishal Naik, headquartered in Los Altos, California. It automatically records, transcribes, and summarizes sales calls, customer success meetings, and other business conversations, then applies conversation intelligence to surface talk-time ratios, sentiment, competitor mentions, objection handling patterns, and topic trends across a team's calls. Avoma includes AI-generated meeting notes and action items, deal collaboration tools, coaching scorecards for managers to evaluate rep performance, and automated CRM logging so call insights and notes sync directly into Salesforce, HubSpot, and other CRM systems. It integrates with video conferencing platforms including Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams, as well as calendar and dialer tools. Avoma offers a freemium pricing model with a free plan for basic call recording and transcription, and paid plans (Starter, Plus, Business, and Enterprise tiers, generally priced per user per month) that unlock conversation intelligence, coaching, forecasting, and advanced CRM integration features. It is aimed at sales, customer success, and revenue operations teams that want to reduce manual note-taking, improve deal visibility, and coach reps using objective conversation data rather than relying on self-reported call summaries.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Avoma is an AI-powered meeting assistant and revenue intelligence platform founded in 2017 and headquartered in Los Altos, California. It records, transcribes, and summarizes sales and customer-facing meetings automatically.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Avoma applies conversation intelligence to surface talk-time ratios, sentiment, competitor mentions, and objection-handling patterns across a team's calls, alongside AI-generated meeting notes and action items.

The platform includes coaching scorecards for managers, deal collaboration tools, and automated CRM logging that syncs call insights into Salesforce, HubSpot, and other CRM systems, plus integrations with Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Avoma offers a free plan covering basic call recording and transcription, with paid Starter, Plus, Business, and Enterprise tiers (generally priced per user per month) unlocking conversation intelligence, coaching, forecasting, and deeper CRM integration.
